items.title: Brokes And Wholes
items.summary: What do I talk about today. I never know. Have I…
items.content_html:
What do I talk about today.

I never know.

Have I run out of things to say?

Maybe.

Maybe.

We all know energy and time are the true forms of currency.

But I'm going to add another.

No, not paper and metal.

I'm going to say your soul.

Your character.

Your integrity.

Your backbone.

Your ability to look yourself in the mirror.

And like who you see.

Not what.

I would say pride but some say that's not a good thing to call it.

🦁

As broke as I've been, I would like to think I never sold that.

Never sold out.

And the crowd goes mild!

When you trade soul for cash, that's the one that breaks you.

And I don't want you to break.

Or be broke, for that matter.

But broke and whole is better than rich and broken.

Make sense?

Make cents?

Make sense.

Make cents.

Look in the mirror.

Right now.

What do you see?

If you can look and smile, deeply, genuinely, you have won.

Man, do I look funny.

😋

And if you cannot, well, there's still time.

You have the energy.

I'm not talking superficial surfaces.

Go inside.

Dig deep.

First principles.

Foundation.

I bet there's something still there.

Untouched.

Unsold.

Good.

And priceless.

To those who struggle, but their souls are intact.

Better broke and whole.

Don't you always say in an A or B choice, to choose C?

Don't be an arse, stay whole.

items.title: It's Not My Fault Being the Biggest and the Strongest. I Don't Even Exercise.
items.summary: I don't lift anymore. Now, if you told me that five…
items.content_html:
I don't lift anymore.

Now, if you told me that five years ago. 

I wouldn't believe you.

I'd say, "That means I'm no longer on this timeline."

And that would be true.

Kinda true.

Almost.

I used to call lifting weights "training."

In that I'd say, "I gotta go train."

Which confused some normal folk.

Silly normal folk.

Train for what?

Nothing.

Just something I do.

But that's what I called it.

I didn't lift iron because I loved it.

Even though I did.

I lifted because I had to take the edge off.

Do you know what I mean?

I had so much fire and fury that I had to spend it on something.

Expend it.

If I didn't, I would explode.

The gym was my sanctuary.

A place I would go to worship, oddly.

A spiritual place.

I would blast my ear drums with special playlists.

My ears thank me today for that.

Thanks, Al.

But I no longer train.

Due to many reasons.

Age.

Near death experiences.

And, strangely, the fire's dimmer.

You could call it getting old(ish).

I'd like to think it's wisdom. Wissmart.

But it's because my body doesn't like lifting anymore.

Or at least right now.

I've punished it for many decades.

It's time to do what it asks.

It's the least I can do, after getting me through all I put it through.

Like an old car you treated like crap, but loved every day.

I built up a suit of armor because I wanted to protect myself.

I suppose there are better ways to do that.

If you'd have told me I don't train anymore, I would have called you a liar.

You might still be one, but not on this.

⛰

"Look, are you just fiddling around with me or what?"

"I just want you to feel you're doing well."

items.title: Head in Alpha Centauri
items.summary: If you told me this is all a dream, I'd believe…
items.content_html:
If you told me this is all a dream, I'd believe you.

That I am dreaming.

Sure, I been through some sheet.

We all have.

And news flash, we'll all go through more.

That's the lens I use now.

Al been through some sheet.

Blessed with a miracle (or three).

For the honor and privilege of going through more sheet.

Oh snap, you bolded that. Must be important.

The honor and privilege of sheet.

More of it.

Sheet, that is.

In case you didn't know.

And so I thank God.

For the challenges.

Because I'm pretty sure I got lucky.

I get lucky.

I am lucky.

Every obstacle.

It's why I'm still here.

Every incident.

Why I still breathe.

Every sheet.

Thank you for letting me be here to help.

I can't bring it like I used to.

I suppose I needed some tuning down, anyways.

But I'm still here, ya'll.

Lost a step, to gain two.

Can't read as well, so I listen more.

Kinda.

Not really.

I can still write.

By touch if needed.

My old law partner used to stand and pace and dictate.

i suppose I could do that.

To ordeals.

And the blessings to be here to face them.

Don't wake me yet.

Life's just getting to the best part.

items.title: I Don't Think You're Ready For This Jelly
items.summary: Marketing. I wrestle with this idea. This idea of marketing. And…
items.content_html:
Marketing.

I wrestle with this idea.

This idea of marketing.

And marketers.

Icky.

And how, so much of their tactical "advice" seems to be manipulative.

Convincing someone they should want something.

Whether they truly want it or not.

And whether or not it would truly add any value (to the buyer).

I always market the other way.

Build it and they will come.

Marketing by not marketing.

This is the way that the experts will tell you will fail.

Because they tend to be all about the numbers.

The braggy numbers.

But here's the thing.

I think I do market.

Just want to do it my way.

You on the fence about buying?

Fine.

Fine.

Go home and think on it.

I ain't begging.

Or twisting your arm.

When I worked at a gym in my youth, we weren't given a commission for memberships. 

So there was no incentive to sign up members.

At least financially.

So I'd just show folks around.

No hard sells.

Just a true enthusiasm for what we offered.

And you know what?

I sold a lot of memberships.

You should have received a commission.

I didn't have to give them a free gimmick.

Or woo them in with introductory prices.

One flat rate.

Discount for seniors and students.

Folks appreciated that.

And that's who I wanted to serve.

Those who disliked being sold.

I closed because I didn't close.

It's always worked.

And it always will.

For some.

This is an interesting filter.

Because some people need to be hard sold.

And some appreciate the opposite.

It is manipulative in the sense that some are wooed by the intrigue of not being sold to.

Who do you want?

Too many of us focus on the numbers.

I think we should focus on the quality of who those numbers represent.

So much of life is getting the decision right once.

And choosing that choice every day of your life.

The amount of trash that becomes something "big" is probably at its all-time high.

Folks out here selling souls.

Their own and others.

But I'm just an old(ish) curmudgeon.

Ain't trying to sell sheet.

For greenbacks or salvation.

But if I was, I'd probably turn a lot of you away.

Terrible marketing, I know.

But eh, what can I say?

items.title: You Name You
items.summary: I don't believe in weak ends. Weekends. I believe in strong…
items.content_html:
I don't believe in weak ends.

Weekends.

I believe in strong beginnings.

I will not go quietly.

What we name things is as important as what they actually are.

I wish it wasn't the case.

Sorta.

I sorta wish that.

That you could call something wonderful a turd and people would still think it wonderful.

But I don't think it's so.

To have a name is to be.

Just make sure you name you.

items.title: Vertical Infinity
items.summary: If you said to me. Al, what time frame defines you?…
items.content_html:
If you said to me.

Al, what time frame defines you?

I'd say back:

Don't call me Al.

Only my friends do.

And I'd go with the 80s.

Growing up in NYC in the 1980s was something special.

Doing it with two constantly working parents made it downright unreal.

Other than school, I was free.

Free range.

My family ran a fruit and vegetables market in Manhattan.

It was really a grocery store, but that's what I called it back then.

I was too young to do much of anything besides get in the way.

But you'd occasionally find me behind the cash register, ringing up people a little too quickly for their liking.

I was just trying to show off.

For the most part, I was free to do anything I wanted.

Within reason.

Don't get in trouble.

Be safe.

And by the grace of God, I didn't.

And I was.

One time, a normally dressed man saw me video gaming in a bodega I hadn't been to before.

He asked me if I was ok.

Which confused me.

Yes, why?

He handed me his card.

NYPD.

If you need help, call me.

I found the whole thing so strange.

Only in looking back, I see the angels.

It wasn't only the boys in blue either.

Some of the infamous folk around town looked out for me, too.

"Leave him alone. He cool."

And so everyone did.

Let me game.

Let me wander.

In peace.

On my adventure.

They protected me in a "Don't turn out like us, little man" way.

Shielding me from reality.

So I could continue living my dream.

As crowned prince of corner video games.

I don't know if that happens anymore.

Moved away from all of its city lights decades ago.

But I hope.

I hope.

No matter what side we're on.

We agree to protect and look out for kids.

To let them be kids for as long as possible.

Within reason.

I didn't leave my heart in the Big Apple.

I took it with me.

New Amsterdam.

And my heart.

But I repeat myself.

As I'm wont to do.

items.title: Exceptions and Rules
items.summary: If you had to pick. Do you pick syllables or letter…
items.content_html:
If you had to pick.

Do you pick syllables or letter count?

What the?

I'm serious.

In a word.

Or a name.

What's more important?

Less syllables or less letters?

I like a many syllable many letter word now and again.

But I am growing to appreciate two syllable words.

Or even phrases.

Two syllable phrases.

Bang bang!

I was an English major, but I couldn't really tell you what an adjective is.

I just know.

I will say, one syllable is too few.

Right?

No.

One letter, also.

Gotta have more.

But not too much.

One of my favorite words has six syllables!

Thirteen letters.

A big word. 

Bigger meaning.

I couldn't tell you what an adjective is.

But I could talk for hours about syllables and letter counts.

Two syllables. Maybe three.

Character count don't really matter.

In this case.

Only this case.

Of course, some of the most important words have one syllable...

Oh, God...

Right. 

Like God.

items.title: You Or Something Like You
items.summary: How soon before we no longer interact with a person, but…
items.content_html:
How soon before we no longer interact with a person, but their ai agent that pretends to be the person?

Emails. Phone calls. Video conferences. Texts.

I'm not even talking about scams.

I'm talking legit people.

Sending out agents to be them.

Meetings seem the obvious choice.

Send your agents to take notes and summarize.

Pointing out relevant sheet.

This could have been an email meeting can now be an email.

Is that happening now?

And can you back out of a deal or something if your agent agrees but you ultimately do not?

Is it binding?

Can you be bound by the actions of your authorized ai agent?

I would think so.

Even, especially, if it goes rogue.

Who are you hiring, the person or the agent?

Will it even matter at a certain point?

I would hope you know I write every dang word here.

No surprise there.

I own it.

Rightly or otherwise.

No machine makes this sheet up.

I'd want my money back if it did.

items.title: Did You Eat?
items.summary: Hello there. Welcome back to this wonderful musings column. Where I…
items.content_html:
Hello there.

Welcome back to this wonderful musings column.

Where I write about whatever I want.

Within reason.

That's what I tell my kids when we go out to eat.

You can have anything you want.

Within reason.

If I were unreasonable, which happens from time to time, this place would be unfit for human consumption.

Maybe ai could eat, but you? 

No.

Unless you're ai.

Then you could eat.

Life is this crazy dance between being reasonable and unreasonable.

Too much reason and you're meh.

Never be meh.

Too much unreason and you're likely shackled.

Never stay caged. Unless you're in a steel cage match, but I digress. Well, actually especially in a steel cage match, because the whole point of it is to win and you can do that by leaving it. But I digress twice.

The joy is in the middle.

Sweet spot.

Goldilocks.

Just right.

Be unreasonable.

Within reason.

items.title: Sniff Test - Pass / Fail (Circle One)
items.summary: What's that called when you attract and repel certain things? Magnetism.
items.content_html:
What's that called when you attract and repel certain things?

Magnetism.

Like typos in scam emails.

Supposedly done intentionally to filter out those who would easily realize it's a scam so that only the remaining susceptible-to-typos folk are left.

I'm not convinced it's intentional, but whatevs.

I don't care.

It works on a lot of levels, too.

Take accolades.

One brags and points to an accomplishment that attracts some people and repels those who know a bit more of what's involved in actually gaining it.

Like those books back in the day that were directories featuring only the best of the best.

Only to find out you just had to buy a listing.

Looks impressive to those who don't know.

"As featured in...."

💨

Creates a shudder in those that do.

Like saying you're a best selling author to the world but really it was in a niche ebook category. 

Or a "true" best selling author but you just bought all of the books yourself.

Not a bad idea.

I ain't trying to hate vehemently dislike.

I'm just telling it like it is. 

Or, rather, as I see it.

Which is how it is.

There are too many people out there.

Gotta narrow it down somehow.

Who you repulse, I'd argue, is even more important than who you attract.

Numbers wise.

Be repulsive to those you don't want.

Attract those that remain.

Everything is a signal.

Even the noise.

Proof's in the pudding.

Whatever that means.*

🧲

Push pull. 

 

* The phrase comes from the 17th-century proverb, "The proof of the pudding is in the eating." Originally, "proof" meant "test". Medieval puddings were savory meat-and-grain sausages. Since meat preservation was unreliable, the only way to test if a pudding was safe and well-made was to eat it.
